stem: An object hangs from a spring. The formula L(w) = 4w + 9 relates the length L, in centimeters, of the spring to the weight w, in newtons, of the object. Which of the following describes the meaning of the 4 in this context?

answerOptions.content: The number of centimeters the spring stretches for each additional newton of weight.
The natural length of the spring, in centimeters, when no weight is attached.
The maximum length, in centimeters, the spring can reach.
The weight, in newtons, of the object hanging from the spring.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. In the linear function L(w) = 4w + 9, the slope 4 represents the change in L per unit increase in w — that is, the number of centimeters the spring stretches for each additional newton of weight.

Choice B describes the constant 9 (the y-intercept). Choices C and D do not correspond to the role of the slope coefficient.
